name: On Holding AG

profile.description: On Holding AG designs, develops, and markets premium performance athletic footwear, apparel, and accessories. Founded in Zurich by former athletes, the company is known for its patented CloudTec cushioning technology, LightSpray robotic manufacturing, and Superfoam materials innovation. On sells through direct-to-consumer channels and wholesale partners globally, competing in the premium running, outdoor, tennis, and lifestyle segments.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): On has delivered one of the most remarkable growth trajectories in athletic footwear history — revenue compounded at approximately 42.8% CAGR from CHF 725M in 2021 to CHF 3,014M in 2025, with Q1 2026 continuing at +26.4% constant currency growth [1][3]. The company successfully expanded from a niche Swiss running brand into a global premium athletic platform spanning running, tennis, outdoor, and lifestyle categories. CloudTec cushioning technology redefined performance running footwear, and LightSpray robotic manufacturing represents a genuine process innovation in shoe construction [2]. AI Deployment (0/2): No specific AI-powered products shipping or quantified AI-driven cost savings reported. The company has not disclosed material AI deployment in its operations or product development [1]. Market Share (2/2): On is the fastest-growing premium athletic footwear brand globally, taking meaningful share from Nike and Adidas in performance running. Revenue surged from near zero to over CHF 3B in a decade, establishing the company as the defining challenger brand in the premium athletic segment [3]. Pipeline (2/2): LightSpray technology scaling across additional shoe models, Superfoam innovations in cushioning materials, aggressive expansion into apparel as a second growth pillar, and a retail store rollout with specific location targets — all with disclosed timelines and investment commitments [2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Significant investment in proprietary materials science (CloudTec, LightSpray, Superfoam) and manufacturing technology relative to footwear industry peers, where most competitors rely on third-party manufacturing and incremental foam chemistry [2].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): On's purpose centers on 'igniting the human spirit through movement' — a specific and thoughtful mission rooted in the founders' personal athletic experience and Swiss innovation DNA [2]. The purpose connects the company's products to a larger vision of human performance and well-being, and it clearly differentiates from generic corporate platitudes. It has not yet been proven through decades of consistency or multi-cycle resilience, preventing a score of 4. Community Impact (0/2): No significant charitable programs, company foundations, or distinctive community investment initiatives were identified in the most recent reporting period. On is still a relatively small company with 3,963 employees scaling rapidly, and community giving programs have not been a public priority [2]. Employee Retention (0/2): No specific employee retention data, average tenure figures, or voluntary turnover rates were found. The company is young and growing rapidly, making retention metrics difficult to assess. Insider Buying (0/2): Co-founders David Allemann and Caspar Coppetti returning as Co-CEOs signals conviction in the company's future direction, but no specific open-market insider purchases with dollar amounts were identified in the last 12 months [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: On Holding's infrastructure profile is exceptional for a growth company. D/E of 30.6% is well below the 70% target, and the company is actually net cash positive with $1.02B cash against $541.9M debt [4]. ROE of 15.5% clears the 15% target. Revenue growth is outstanding — 14.5% reported YoY (26.4% constant currency) with a 5-year CAGR of approximately 42.8% [1][3]. Forward P/E of 17.38x is below the 20x target, representing a rare combination of high growth at a reasonable forward valuation. Forward PEG of 0.72 is well below the 1.0 target, confirming the growth-adjusted valuation is attractive [4]. The primary drag is profit margin at 8.02%, roughly 40% of the way to the 20% target — though this is improving rapidly as Q1 2026 gross margin hit 64.2% and adjusted EBITDA margin reached 21.0% [1]. Free cash flow of $240M demonstrates the business is self-funding its growth. Liquidity floor easily cleared at approximately $229.6M daily ($37.07 x 6.19M shares) [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): On Holding's core business — designing, manufacturing, and selling physical footwear and apparel — is structurally protected from AI disruption. Shoes require materials science, manufacturing precision, physical retail experiences, and brand loyalty that AI cannot replicate [1]. The direct-to-consumer retail expansion creates physical touchpoints that are inherently AI-resistant. Customer purchase decisions in premium athletic footwear are driven by fit, feel, performance, and brand identity — none of which AI can substitute. However, On does not operate in a deeply regulated or essential infrastructure category (unlike aerospace or utilities), and AI-powered design tools could eventually accelerate competitors' product development cycles, preventing an IMMUNE rating. Analysts have not flagged AI disruption as a meaningful risk to On's business model [4]. AI Opportunity (0/1): On does not generate quantifiable revenue from AI-related products or services. While AI could potentially assist with supply chain optimization, demand forecasting, or personalized marketing, no specific AI revenue contribution or quantified AI-driven savings have been disclosed [1].

infinite_game.summary: On Holding exhibits genuine infinite game characteristics — a founder-led company built around authentic product innovation and a clear purpose rooted in athletic performance. The co-founders' return as Co-CEOs reinforces the connection between purpose and leadership. However, the company is young (founded 2010, IPO 2021) and has not yet been tested through a full economic cycle or leadership transition crisis. Its category expansions are promising but incremental rather than transformative. The competitive approach — innovating past Nike and Adidas rather than attacking them — reflects infinite thinking, though the company's finite age limits how much endurance can be demonstrated.

just_cause.analysis: On was founded in Zurich by former Ironman champion Olivier Bernhard and friends David Allemann and Caspar Coppetti, driven by an authentic desire to create a better running shoe through Swiss engineering innovation [2]. The purpose of 'igniting the human spirit through movement' is genuine and has guided 14 years of consistent product innovation — from the first CloudTec running shoe to LightSpray manufacturing technology to expansion into tennis, outdoor, and lifestyle. The purpose shapes product decisions and brand identity. However, On's cause, while authentic, is fundamentally product-obsession — a passion for building the perfect running shoe — rather than Sinek's definition of a Just Cause that envisions a future state so appealing people will sacrifice to advance it. The company is too young to have proven this purpose through crisis or multi-decade consistency. (2/3 points)

courage_to_lead.analysis: On has demonstrated meaningful courage in its investment decisions — pouring resources into the proprietary LightSpray robotic manufacturing technology and Superfoam materials innovation rather than relying on commodity contract manufacturing like most competitors [2]. The aggressive expansion into apparel and retail stores while still primarily a shoe company represents a willingness to invest ahead of proven demand. The co-founders' decision to return as Co-CEOs rather than hiring an outside executive signals commitment to the original vision over conventional corporate governance. However, these are forward-leaning growth investments during a period of exceptional momentum — the true test of courageous leadership will come during the company's first real downturn. (1/2 points)

trusting_teams.analysis: On maintains a small, highly productive team of 3,963 employees delivering CHF 3B+ in revenue — approximately CHF 760K revenue per employee, which is exceptional for the footwear industry [3]. The founder-led culture with co-founders returning as Co-CEOs, combined with the internal promotion of Scott Maguire to President & COO after leading the LightSpray technology scale-up, suggests a meritocratic environment that rewards execution [2]. The Swiss corporate culture emphasizes collaboration and precision. However, the company is young with limited track record through adversity, and no specific employee satisfaction data, voluntary turnover rates, or cultural practices demonstrating psychological safety were identified. (1/2 points)

existential_flexibility.analysis: On has shown meaningful strategic evolution — expanding from pure running shoes into tennis (partnering with Roger Federer), outdoor performance, lifestyle/fashion, and now apparel and owned retail stores [2][3]. The DTC pivot from wholesale-dependent distribution represents a significant business model evolution. Each expansion was guided by the core innovation purpose rather than opportunism. However, these are adjacent category expansions and distribution channel shifts — incremental evolution rather than the transformative reinvention Sinek describes (abandoning a core business entirely in service of the Just Cause). The company has not yet faced a situation requiring true existential flexibility. (1/2 points)

worthy_rivals.analysis: On's competitive approach exemplifies Sinek's worthy rival concept. Rather than attacking Nike and Adidas as enemies to defeat, On treats their dominance as motivation to innovate — competing through technological differentiation (CloudTec, LightSpray) and premium brand positioning rather than price wars or market share attacks [2][3]. Management language consistently focuses on what On is building rather than what competitors are doing wrong. The company's respect for Nike's brand power and Adidas's heritage while charting its own distinct path through innovation reflects an infinite competitive mindset. (1/1 point)

investment_thesis: On Holding is the fastest-growing premium athletic brand in the world, delivering CHF 3,014M in FY2025 revenue (+30.0%) with Q1 2026 constant currency growth accelerating to 26.4% and full-year 2026 guidance of at least 23% constant currency growth [1][3]. The company's financial profile is rapidly improving — Q1 2026 gross margin expanded 430bps to 64.2%, adjusted EBITDA margin reached 21.0% (up from 16.5% YoY), and net income surged 82.2% [1]. At 17.38x forward P/E with a 0.72 PEG ratio, the stock offers rare growth-at-a-reasonable-price in the consumer discretionary space [4]. The balance sheet is fortress-like: $1.02B cash against $541.9M debt, net cash positive, with $240M in free cash flow funding growth without dilution. CloudTec and LightSpray represent genuine technological moats in an industry dominated by incremental foam chemistry. The DTC expansion, apparel growth pillar, and retail store rollout provide multiple vectors for sustained 20%+ revenue growth through the decade [2][3].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on consumer cyclicality and execution risk at scale. On's premium positioning ($150-250 price points) makes it acutely vulnerable to consumer spending pullbacks — during a recession, discretionary athletic footwear purchases are among the first items cut, and the brand has never been tested through a downturn [4]. Profit margin of 8.02% remains thin for a premium brand, and the gap between gross margin (64.2%) and net margin suggests significant operating expense growth as the company scales its DTC infrastructure, retail stores, and global headcount [1]. Significant Swiss franc appreciation versus USD creates persistent translation headwinds — Q1 2026 reported growth of 14.5% versus 26.4% constant currency illustrates the magnitude of this risk [1]. The CEO transition (Martin Hoffmann departing, co-founders assuming dual-CEO structure) introduces governance risk, as dual-CEO arrangements have a mixed track record. At $12.4B market cap, the stock prices in continued exceptional execution — any deceleration in growth or margin expansion would compress the multiple significantly. Competition from Nike's innovation reset and emerging brands like Hoka creates category crowding risk [3][4].
key_catalysts: Gross margin expansion toward 65%+ as LightSpray manufacturing scales and DTC mix increases — Q1 2026 already reached 64.2%, up 430bps YoY, with full-year guidance of at least 64.5% [1]
Apparel category expansion as a second growth pillar — On is leveraging its brand equity and DTC infrastructure to build a multi-billion-dollar apparel business alongside footwear [2][3]

Constant currency revenue growth of 23%+ guided for FY2026 with margin leverage driving net income growth well above revenue growth, as demonstrated by Q1's 82.2% net income surge on 26.4% CC revenue growth [1][3]
key_risks: Consumer cyclicality exposure — premium $150-250 athletic footwear is highly discretionary, and On has never navigated a recession as a public company, leaving downside earnings sensitivity unknown [4]

Swiss franc appreciation creating persistent translation headwinds — Q1 2026 showed a 12 percentage point gap between reported (14.5%) and constant currency (26.4%) growth, compressing reported results despite strong underlying demand [1]

CEO transition and dual-CEO governance risk — co-founders David Allemann and Caspar Coppetti assuming Co-CEO roles from departing CEO Martin Hoffmann introduces an unconventional leadership structure with limited precedent for sustained success [2]
